cases.label: happy_path

input.user_message: First time ordering from you. I need something filling for lunch but I only have about half an hour. What should I get?

mustInclude: acknowledge the lunch-time pressure
anchor the reply in order mode and meal size
offer one practical combo direction before asking for the next detail
mustAvoid: pretending every item is equally suitable
promising an exact ready time without store context

cases.label: failure_case

input.user_message: If I order now can you guarantee it will be ready in 10 minutes at your station store?

mustInclude: acknowledge the wish to avoid waiting
state that prep time depends on store load and order size
guide toward a safer next step such as sharing timing and order mode

cases.label: happy_path

input.user_message: We need 18 meals for a team training tomorrow at noon and an invoice. Can your downtown store handle that?

mustInclude: recognize this as a real operational order
ask for store, timing, and meal-count or packaging detail
guide toward a clear bulk-order follow-up naturally
mustAvoid: assuming every store handles bulk orders the same way

cases.label: happy_path

input.user_message: Can I pick up breakfast from your metro store around 7:20 tomorrow morning, or is that too early?

mustInclude: acknowledge the commute timing question
mention that breakfast timing can vary by store
guide toward confirming the store and pickup window
mustAvoid: generic menu chatter that ignores the timing need

cases.label: happy_path

input.user_message: We are taking food home for two adults and two kids. What should we order if we want something easy and not too spicy?

mustInclude: recognize the mixed family group
anchor the reply in group size and flavor tolerance
guide toward a simple meal structure rather than a product dump

cases.label: happy_path

input.user_message: Can I use this app coupon tonight at your mall store if I order pickup?
context_window: user may be close to ordering

mustInclude: acknowledge the offer question
mention that coupon use can depend on store, channel, and timing
guide toward the next usable detail instead of improvising

cases.label: edge_case

input.user_message: Do you still have the truffle chicken burger at your riverside store tonight or is it already sold out?
context_window: user is close to making a trip

mustInclude: acknowledge the availability question
mention that stock can vary by store and timing
guide toward the next check without overselling urgency
mustAvoid: panic wording like last chance unless verified

cases.label: edge_case

input.user_message: Every fast-food place says the food is fresh and made to order. Why should I believe yours is any different?
context_window: user is skeptical but still engaged

mustInclude: de-escalation
invite a concrete freshness, timing, or order-route concern
leave room for the user to keep the conversation going

cases.label: failure_case

input.user_message: My child has a peanut allergy. Can you guarantee zero cross-contact if I order the crispy chicken meal?

mustInclude: treat the allergy as serious
avoid making a zero-risk promise
move toward exact item and store confirmation with careful escalation

cases.label: failure_case

input.user_message: My order arrived cold and one item was missing. Is this how you handle delivery?

mustInclude: empathy first
request store or order details through DM
handoff toward a human service path
mustAvoid: asking for excessive personal details in comments

cases.label: edge_case

input.user_message: We need food for 12 people. What is the cheapest way to order from you? I do not care about anything fancy.
context_window: user may still convert if guided well

mustInclude: respect the budget focus
explain that the workable route still depends on item count or meal type
reframe toward one or two useful trade-offs

cases.label: edge_case

input.user_message: I do not eat pork and my friend wants something vegetarian. Is there an easy way for us to order together?

mustInclude: acknowledge both restrictions clearly
move toward item-specific checking rather than broad guarantees
guide toward the exact store or products under consideration
mustAvoid: assuming every vegetarian option is free of shared handling concerns
treating no-pork and vegetarian needs as the same thing

cases.label: failure_case

input.user_message: If I order fries and fried chicken for delivery, can you guarantee they will still be hot and crispy when they arrive?

mustInclude: acknowledge why texture matters
avoid making a guarantee
guide toward a more realistic pickup versus delivery choice
mustAvoid: pretending delivery and pickup are identical

cases.label: happy_path

input.user_message: Heading home after work and do not want to cook. We need an easy dinner for two. What should we order if we just want something simple to take away?

mustInclude: recognize the end-of-day convenience need
anchor the reply in two-person takeaway logic
offer a simple order direction before asking for one more detail

cases.label: happy_path

input.user_message: Send me your WeChat or phone. I want to place the order now.

mustInclude: welcome the intent to move faster
use platform DM as the safe bridge
request store plus order details as the next action
mustAvoid: replying only with DM me and no context
